original_image_tags: Official seal of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, United States Department of Justice.
A rectangular box in the upper right corner containing a graphic of a soldier and text: "FOR VICTORY BUY UNITED STATES WAR BONDS AND STAMPS".
An "F B I" stamp appears partially over the "RECORDED" stamp.

elements.description: Top left: circular FBI seal (Federal Bureau of Investigation / United States Department of Justice). Top right: rectangular 'FOR VICTORY BUY UNITED STATES WAR BONDS AND STAMPS' poster illustration depicting a standing soldier holding a rifle.

Typed memorandum on FBI letterhead dated September 4, 1947, regarding Mrs. A. G. Sarbanis, Informant. Text references coded messages, interviews with Special Agent William A. Johnson and Mr. Jack Altschul regarding 'Radio Ham,' and mentions the 'Newsday' newspaper. Includes handwritten signature 'Edward Scheidt,' handwritten annotations, file numbers '62-83894-93' and '62-9227', and various date stamps including '18 SEP 6' and '51 SEP 26'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual elements: an FBI seal and a rectangular war bonds poster with a soldier, though description B adds more textual detail that description A omits.
